name: Geometry.Expect
comment:
This module contains functions that construct [`Expectation`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm-explorations/test/latest/Expect)s
for `elm-geometry` types. In general, all expectations use an
absolute-or-relative tolerance of 1e-12 meters; for example,

    actualPoint |> Expect.point2d expectedPoint

will pass if the distance from `actualPoint` to `expectedPoint` is less than
1e-12 meters _or_ if it is less than 1e-12 times the distance of `actualPoint`
from the origin (so that a larger absolute tolerance is used for points far from
the origin).

This module has been designed so that in most cases you can use

    import Geometry.Expect as Expect

to 'merge' it with the `Expect` module from `elm-explorations/test`, without
running into any naming conflicts; examples in this module assume the module has
been imported this way.


# Generic helpers

@docs exactly, just, list


# `Quantity` equality

@docs quantity, quantityWithin, angle, angleWithin


# `Quantity` comparison

These functions all behave just like [the corresponding ones in
`elm-explorations/test`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm-explorations/test/latest/Expect#numeric-comparisons).
Unlike most other functions in this module, they do _not_ implicitly include a
tolerance.

@docs quantityAtLeast, quantityAtMost, quantityGreaterThan, quantityLessThan


# Intervals

@docs quantityContainedIn


# Points

@docs point2d, point2dWithin, point2dContainedIn, point3d, point3dWithin, point3dContainedIn


# Vectors

@docs vector2d, vector2dWithin, vector2dContainedIn, vector3d, vector3dWithin, vector3dContainedIn


# Directions

@docs direction2d, direction2dWithin, direction2dPerpendicularTo, direction3d, direction3dWithin, direction3dPerpendicularTo


# Bounding boxes

@docs boundingBox2d, boundingBox2dWithin, boundingBox3d, boundingBox3dWithin


# Simple geometry

@docs lineSegment2d, lineSegment2dWithin, lineSegment3d, lineSegment3dWithin, triangle2d, triangle2dWithin, triangle3d, triangle3dWithin, polyline2d, polyline2dWithin, polyline3d, polyline3dWithin, polygon2d, polygon2dWithin


# Complex geometry

@docs arc2d, arc3d, circle2d, circle3d, cubicSpline2d, cubicSpline3d, cylinder3d, cone3d, quadraticSpline2d, quadraticSpline3d, sphere3d


# Datums

@docs axis2d, axis3d, frame2d, frame3d, plane3d, sketchPlane3d


# Advanced

These functions check for things like "directions have length 1", "bounding box
min and max values are in the correct order", and "frame basis directions are
mutually perpendicular". Since these properties are generally guaranteed by
`elm-geometry`, it should only be necessary to use these expectation functions
if you have some low-level code that calls functions such as
`Direction2d.unsafe` or `Frame3d.unsafe`.

@docs validDirection2d, validDirection3d, validBoundingBox2d, validBoundingBox3d, validFrame2d, validFrame3d, validSketchPlane3d

values.name: angle
values.comment:
Check that one `Angle` is approximately equal to another.

Note that this will consider angles in the geometric sense, not the numerical
sense; 0 degrees, 360 degrees, 720 degrees and -360 degrees are all considered
to be equal. However, since `Angle` values are also `Quantity` values, you can
use `Expect.quantity` if you _do_ want a pure numerical comparison.

values.name: angleWithin
values.comment:
Check if two angles are equal within a given tolerance. Note that as with
`angle`, this will properly handle angles that 'wrap around':

    -- Passes because geometrically speaking, there's only
    -- a 4 degree difference between 358 degrees and 2
    -- degrees:
    Angle.degrees 358
        |> Expect.angleWithin (Angle.degrees 5)
            (Angle.degrees 2)

values.name: arc2d
values.comment:
Check that two `Arc2d` values are approximately equal (have the same
start point, end point and swept angle).
values.type: Arc2d.Arc2d units coordinates -> Arc2d.Arc2d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: arc3d
values.comment:
Check that two `Arc3d` values are approximately equal (have the same
start point, end point, swept angle and axial direction).
values.type: Arc3d.Arc3d units coordinates -> Arc3d.Arc3d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: axis2d
values.comment:
Check that two `Axis2d` values are approximately equal (have the same
origin point and direction).
values.type: Axis2d.Axis2d units coordinates -> Axis2d.Axis2d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: axis3d
values.comment:
Check that two `Axis3d` values are approximately equal (have the same
origin point and direction).
values.type: Axis3d.Axis3d units coordinates -> Axis3d.Axis3d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: boundingBox2d
values.comment:
Check that two `BoundingBox2d` values are approximately equal (have the same
min/max values).
values.type: BoundingBox2d.BoundingBox2d units coordinates -> BoundingBox2d.BoundingBox2d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: boundingBox2dWithin
values.comment:
Check that two `BoundingBox2d` values are equal within the given tolerance
(every min/max value of the first box is equal, to within the given tolerance,
of the corresponding min/max value of the second box).
values.type: Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> BoundingBox2d.BoundingBox2d units coordinates -> BoundingBox2d.BoundingBox2d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: boundingBox3d
values.comment:
Check that two `BoundingBox3d` values are approximately equal (have the same
min/max values).
values.type: BoundingBox3d.BoundingBox3d units coordinates -> BoundingBox3d.BoundingBox3d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: boundingBox3dWithin
values.comment:
Check that two `BoundingBox3d` values are equal within the given tolerance
(every min/max value of the first box is equal, to within the given tolerance,
of the corresponding min/max value of the second box).
values.type: Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> BoundingBox3d.BoundingBox3d units coordinates -> BoundingBox3d.BoundingBox3d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: circle2d
values.comment:
Check that two `Circle2d` values are approximately equal (have the same
center point and radius).
values.type: Circle2d.Circle2d units coordinates -> Circle2d.Circle2d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: circle3d
values.comment:
Check that two `Circle3d` values are approximately equal (have the same
center point, axial direction and radius).
values.type: Circle3d.Circle3d units coordinates -> Circle3d.Circle3d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: cone3d
values.comment:
Check that two `Cone3d` values are approximately equal (have the same
center point, axial direction, radius and length).
values.type: Cone3d.Cone3d units coordinates -> Cone3d.Cone3d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: cubicSpline2d
values.comment:
Check that two `CubicSpline2d` values are approximately equal (have the
same control points).
values.type: CubicSpline2d.CubicSpline2d units coordinates -> CubicSpline2d.CubicSpline2d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: cubicSpline3d
values.comment:
Check that two `CubicSpline3d` values are approximately equal (have the
same control points).
values.type: CubicSpline3d.CubicSpline3d units coordinates -> CubicSpline3d.CubicSpline3d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: cylinder3d
values.comment:
Check that two `Cylinder3d` values are approximately equal (have the same
center point, axial direction, radius and length).
values.type: Cylinder3d.Cylinder3d units coordinates -> Cylinder3d.Cylinder3d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: direction2d
values.comment: Check that two `Direction2d` values are approximately equal.

values.name: direction2dPerpendicularTo
values.comment: Check that one `Direction2d` is approximately perpendicular to another.

values.name: direction2dWithin
values.comment:
Check that two `Direction2d` values are equal to within the given angular
tolerance.
values.type: Angle.Angle -> Direction2d.Direction2d coordinates -> Direction2d.Direction2d coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: direction3d
values.comment: Check that two `Direction3d` values are approximately equal.

values.name: direction3dPerpendicularTo
values.comment: Check that one `Direction3d` is approximately perpendicular to another.

values.name: direction3dWithin
values.comment:
Check that two `Direction3d` values are equal to within the given angular
tolerance.
values.type: Angle.Angle -> Direction3d.Direction3d coordinates -> Direction3d.Direction3d coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: exactly
values.comment:
Check if one `Float` value is _exactly_ equal to another.

Note that this is usually only desirable in low-level numeric code and most
tests should use [`Expect.float`](#float).

values.name: frame2d
values.comment:
Check that two `Frame2d` values are approximately equal (have the same
origin point and X/Y directions).
values.type: Frame2d.Frame2d units coordinates defines -> Frame2d.Frame2d units coordinates defines -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: frame3d
values.comment:
Check that two `Frame3d` values are approximately equal (have the same
origin point and X/Y/Z directions).
values.type: Frame3d.Frame3d units coordinates defines -> Frame3d.Frame3d units coordinates defines -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: just
values.comment:
Check if a `Maybe` value is of the form `Just x` and `x` satisfies the given
expectation function. For example, since `Vector2d.direction` returns a `Maybe
Direction2d`, you might test it like

    Vector2d.meters 2 2
        |> Vector2d.direction
        |> Expect.just
            (Expect.direction2d (Direction2d.degrees 45))

Note that if you want to verify that a function returns `Nothing`, you can
simply use [`Expect.equal`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm-explorations/test/latest/Expect#equal):

    Vector2d.meters 0 0
        |> Vector2d.direction
        |> Expect.equal Nothing

values.name: lineSegment2d
values.comment:
Check that two `LineSegment2d` values are approximately equal (have the same
endpoints).
values.type: LineSegment2d.LineSegment2d units coordinates -> LineSegment2d.LineSegment2d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: lineSegment2dWithin
values.comment:
Check that two `LineSegment2d` values are equal within the given tolerance
(the endpoints of one are equal to the endpoints of the other, within the given
tolerance).
values.type: Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> LineSegment2d.LineSegment2d units coordinates -> LineSegment2d.LineSegment2d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: lineSegment3d
values.comment:
Check that two `LineSegment3d` values are approximately equal (have the same
endpoints).
values.type: LineSegment3d.LineSegment3d units coordinates -> LineSegment3d.LineSegment3d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: lineSegment3dWithin
values.comment:
Check that two `LineSegment3d` values are equal within the given tolerance
(the endpoints of one are equal to the endpoints of the other, within the given
tolerance).
values.type: Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> LineSegment3d.LineSegment3d units coordinates -> LineSegment3d.LineSegment3d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: list
values.comment:
Apply a given expectation function to successive pairs of items from two given lists, failing
if any one of those expectations fails or if the two lists have different lengths.
values.type: (a -> b -> Expect.Expectation) -> List.List a -> List.List b -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: plane3d
values.comment:
Check that two `Plane3d` values are approximately equal (have the same
origin point and normal direction).
values.type: Plane3d.Plane3d units coordinates -> Plane3d.Plane3d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: point2d
values.comment: Check that two `Point2d` values are approximately equal.
values.type: Point2d.Point2d units coordinates -> Point2d.Point2d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: point2dContainedIn
values.comment:
Check that a `Point2d` is approximately contained in a given
`BoundingBox2d`.
values.type: BoundingBox2d.BoundingBox2d units coordinates -> Point2d.Point2d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: point2dWithin
values.comment:
Check that two `Point2d` values are equal to within the given tolerance
(the distance between the two points is less than that tolerance).
values.type: Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> Point2d.Point2d units coordinates -> Point2d.Point2d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: point3d
values.comment: Check that two `Point3d` values are approximately equal.
values.type: Point3d.Point3d units coordinates -> Point3d.Point3d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: point3dContainedIn
values.comment:
Check that a `Point3d` is approximately contained in a given
`BoundingBox3d`.
values.type: BoundingBox3d.BoundingBox3d units coordinates -> Point3d.Point3d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: point3dWithin
values.comment:
Check that two `Point3d` values are equal to within the given tolerance
(the distance between the two points is less than that tolerance).
values.type: Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> Point3d.Point3d units coordinates -> Point3d.Point3d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: polygon2d
values.comment:
Check that two `Polygon2d` values are approximately equal (have the same
vertices).
values.type: Polygon2d.Polygon2d units coordinates -> Polygon2d.Polygon2d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: polygon2dWithin
values.comment:
Check that two `Polygon2d` values are equal within the given tolerance (have
the same number of vertices, and each vertex of the first polygon is equal to
the corresponding vertex of the second polygon within the given tolerance).
values.type: Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> Polygon2d.Polygon2d units coordinates -> Polygon2d.Polygon2d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: polyline2d
values.comment:
Check that two `Polyline2d` values are approximately equal (have the same
vertices).
values.type: Polyline2d.Polyline2d units coordinates -> Polyline2d.Polyline2d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: polyline2dWithin
values.comment:
Check that two `Polyline2d` values are equal within the given tolerance
(have the same number of vertices, and each vertex of the first polyline is
equal to the corresponding vertex of the second polyline within the given
tolerance).
values.type: Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> Polyline2d.Polyline2d units coordinates -> Polyline2d.Polyline2d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: polyline3d
values.comment:
Check that two `Polyline3d` values are approximately equal (have the same
vertices).
values.type: Polyline3d.Polyline3d units coordinates -> Polyline3d.Polyline3d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: polyline3dWithin
values.comment:
Check that two `Polyline3d` values are equal within the given tolerance
(have the same number of vertices, and each vertex of the first polyline is
equal to the corresponding vertex of the second polyline within the given
tolerance).
values.type: Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> Polyline3d.Polyline3d units coordinates -> Polyline3d.Polyline3d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: quadraticSpline2d
values.comment:
Check that two `QuadraticSpline2d` values are approximately equal (have the
same control points).
values.type: QuadraticSpline2d.QuadraticSpline2d units coordinates -> QuadraticSpline2d.QuadraticSpline2d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: quadraticSpline3d
values.comment:
Check that two `QuadraticSpline3d` values are approximately equal (have the
same control points).
values.type: QuadraticSpline3d.QuadraticSpline3d units coordinates -> QuadraticSpline3d.QuadraticSpline3d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: quantity
values.comment:
Check that one [`Quantity`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/ianmackenzie/elm-units/latest/Quantity)
value is approximately equal to another.
values.type: Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: quantityAtLeast
values.type: Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: quantityAtMost
values.type: Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: quantityContainedIn
values.comment:
Check whether a given quantity is contained within a given interval, within the default
tolerance of 1e-12 units. (That is, a quantity _just_ outside the interval will still be considered
contained in the interval.)
values.type: Quantity.Interval.Interval Basics.Float units -> Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: quantityGreaterThan
values.type: Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: quantityLessThan
values.type: Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: quantityWithin
values.comment:
Check that some `Quantity` value is equal to another within a specified
absolute tolerance:

    actualValue
        |> Expect.quantityWithin givenTolerance
            expectedValue

    -- Passes:
    Length.meters 1.05
        |> Expect.quantityWithin (Length.centimeters 10)
            (Length.meters 1)

    -- Fails:
    Length.meters 1.05
        |> Expect.quantityWithin (Length.centimeters 1)
            (Length.meters 1)
values.type: Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: sketchPlane3d
values.comment:
Check that two `SketchPlane3d` values are approximately equal (have the same
origin point and X/Y directions).
values.type: SketchPlane3d.SketchPlane3d units coordinates defines -> SketchPlane3d.SketchPlane3d units coordinates defines -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: sphere3d
values.comment:
Check that two `Sphere3d` values are approximately equal (have the same
center point and radius).
values.type: Sphere3d.Sphere3d units coordinates -> Sphere3d.Sphere3d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: triangle2d
values.comment:
Check that two `Triangle2d` values are approximately equal (have the same
vertices).
values.type: Triangle2d.Triangle2d units coordinates -> Triangle2d.Triangle2d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: triangle2dWithin
values.comment:
Check that two `Triangle2d` values are equal within the given tolerance
(each vertex of the first triangle is equal to the corresponding vertex of the
second triangle, within the given tolerance).
values.type: Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> Triangle2d.Triangle2d units coordinates -> Triangle2d.Triangle2d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: triangle3d
values.comment:
Check that two `Triangle3d` values are approximately equal (have the same
vertices).
values.type: Triangle3d.Triangle3d units coordinates -> Triangle3d.Triangle3d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: triangle3dWithin
values.comment:
Check that two `Triangle3d` values are equal within the given tolerance
(each vertex of the first triangle is equal to the corresponding vertex of the
second triangle, within the given tolerance).
values.type: Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> Triangle3d.Triangle3d units coordinates -> Triangle3d.Triangle3d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: vector2d
values.comment: Check that two `Vector2d` values are approximately equal.
values.type: Vector2d.Vector2d units coordinates -> Vector2d.Vector2d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: vector2dContainedIn
values.comment:
Check that a `Vector2d` is approximately contained in a given
`VectorBoundingBox2d`.
values.type: VectorBoundingBox2d.VectorBoundingBox2d units coordinates -> Vector2d.Vector2d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: vector2dWithin
values.comment:
Check that two `Vector2d` values are equal to within the given tolerance
(the difference between the two vectors has magnitude less than that tolerance).
values.type: Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> Vector2d.Vector2d units coordinates -> Vector2d.Vector2d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: vector3d
values.comment: Check that two `Vector3d` values are approximately equal.
values.type: Vector3d.Vector3d units coordinates -> Vector3d.Vector3d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: vector3dContainedIn
values.comment:
Check that a `Vector3d` is approximately contained in a given
`VectorBoundingBox3d`.
values.type: VectorBoundingBox3d.VectorBoundingBox3d units coordinates -> Vector3d.Vector3d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

values.name: vector3dWithin
values.comment:
Check that two `Vector3d` values are equal to within the given tolerance
(the difference between the two vectors has magnitude less than that tolerance).
values.type: Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float units -> Vector3d.Vector3d units coordinates -> Vector3d.Vector3d units coordinates -> Expect.Expectation

name: Geometry.Random
comment:
Random generators for lots of different geometric types, intended for use in tests. 10 meters is
(arbitrarily) used as the basic scale for generated values, so for example the `point3d` generator
will generate points where each coordinate value is in the range -10 meters to +10 meters.

This is only to have values be roughly the same scale (which helps to ensure that random tests
actually do trigger things like intersections between objects) but do not rely on geometry being
strictly contained within the -10...+10 meter bounds. For example, the `circle2d` generator will
create circles where the _center_ point is within those bounds, and the radius is up to 10 meters,
meaning that the circle may well extend to larger or smaller coordinate values.

Most of the higher-level generators in this module are built pretty directly from lower-level
generators - for example the `axis3d` generator is implemented as:

    axis3d : Generator (Axis3d Meters coordinates)
    axis3d =
        Random.map2 Axis3d.through point3d direction3d

@docs parameterValue, scale, length, positiveLength, angle, unitlessQuantity, unitlessInterval


# Primitives

@docs direction2d, direction3d, vector2d, vector3d, point2d, point3d


## Bounding boxes

@docs boundingBox2d, boundingBox3d, vectorBoundingBox2d, vectorBoundingBox3d

@docs axis2d, axis3d, plane3d, sketchPlane3d, frame2d, frame3d


# Geometry

@docs lineSegment2d, lineSegment3d, triangle2d, triangle3d, rectangle2d, rectangle3d, polygon2d, block3d


## Arcs, circles, ellipses

@docs arc2d, arc3d, circle2d, circle3d, ellipticalArc2d, ellipticalArc3d, ellipse2d


## Spline curves

@docs quadraticSpline2d, quadraticSpline3d, cubicSpline2d, cubicSpline3d, rationalQuadraticSpline2d, rationalQuadraticSpline3d, rationalCubicSpline2d, rationalCubicSpline3d, spline2d, spline3d


## 3D solids

@docs sphere3d, cylinder3d, cone3d, ellipsoid3d


# Utilities

@docs smallList

values.name: angle
values.comment: Generate a random angle between -360 and +360 degrees.

values.name: direction2d
values.comment: Generate a random 2D direction.

values.name: direction3d
values.comment: Generate a random 3D direction.

values.name: length
values.comment: Generate a random length between -10 meters and +10 meters.

values.name: parameterValue
values.comment: Generate a parameter value between 0 and 1.

values.name: point2d
values.comment: Generate a random 2D point with components in the range -10 to +10 meters.

values.name: point3d
values.comment: Generate a random 3D point with components in the range -10 to +10 meters.

values.name: polygon2d
values.comment:
The `polygon2d` generator is a bit special. Since randomly generating vertices and then
connecting them would almost always result in useless self-intersecting polygons, this instead
generates polygons of a few different types that are non-self-intersecting by construction:

  - A donut-shaped polygon with a hole in the middle
  - An L-shaped polygon
  - A roughly square-shaped polygon
  - A few variations on square-ish polygons with one or two holes

All the polygons will be roughly centered on the origin.

values.name: positiveLength
values.comment: Generate a random length between 0 and 10 meters.

values.name: scale
values.comment: Generate a value between -10 and +10 to use for scaling geometry. (Note that this includes 0.)

values.name: smallList
values.comment: Generate a list of 0 to 32 elements, using the given generator for items.

values.name: unitlessInterval
values.comment: Generate a random unitless interval with endpoints between -10 and +10.

values.name: unitlessQuantity
values.comment: Generate a random unitless quantity between -10 and +10.

values.name: vector2d
values.comment: Generate a random 2D vector with components in the range -10 to +10 meters.

values.name: vector3d
values.comment: Generate a random 3D vector with components in the range -10 to +10 meters.
